seoTitle: Pine Ridge vs Piney Range — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pine Ridge and Piney Range. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pine Ridge vs Piney Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pine Ridge and Piney Range.
heading: Pine Ridge vs Piney Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pine Ridge, New South Wales and Piney Range, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Piney Range has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Pine Ridge (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pine Ridge or Piney Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pine Ridge has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Piney Range scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pine Ridge scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Piney Range scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pine Ridge or Piney Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Piney Range has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
